# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent {
        label 'master'
    }

    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                echo 'Checking out source code...'
                // Example: replace with your SCM checkout step
                // git branch: 'main', credentialsId: 'your-git-credentials-id', url: 'https://github.com/your-org/your-repo.git'
            }
        }
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                echo 'Building application...'
                // Example: replace with your build commands
                // sh 'mvn clean install -DskipTests'
                // sh './gradlew build -x test'
                // sh 'npm install && npm run build'
            }
        }
        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                echo 'Running tests...'
                // Example: replace with your test commands
                // sh 'mvn test'
                // sh './gradlew test'
                // sh 'npm test'
            }
        }
        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                echo 'Deploying to production...'
                // Example: replace with your deployment commands
                // withCredentials([usernamePassword(credentialsId: 'prod-ssh-credentials', usernameVariable: 'PROD_USER', passwordVariable: 'PROD_PASS')]) {
                //     sh "scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no target/your-app.jar ${PROD_USER}@your-prod-server:/opt/your-app/"
                //     sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${PROD_USER}@your-prod-server 'sudo systemctl restart your-app'"
                // }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            echo 'Pipeline finished.'
        }
        success {
            echo 'Deployment successful!'
        }
        failure {
            echo 'Deployment failed!'
        }
    }
}
